items.question_text: The `apt` command is documented as a high-level command-line interface intended for end users.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt upgrade` command only refreshes package metadata and does not install package upgrades.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt upgrade` command does not remove existing installed packages.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt full-upgrade` command never removes installed packages.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: Removing a package with `apt remove` can leave modified user configuration files behind.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt autoremove` command removes only packages that the user explicitly installed by hand.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt search` command can search available packages for regex terms and display matches.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt list` command can show only installed packages, not upgradeable or all available versions.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt edit-sources` command edits `sources.list` files and provides basic sanity checks.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get update` command installs every upgradable package on the system.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get` documentation says `update` should be performed before `upgrade` or `dist-upgrade`.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get install` command never retrieves dependency packages automatically.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get remove` command removes a package but leaves configuration files on the system.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get purge` command keeps configuration files in place so the package can be restored quickly.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get download` command downloads the requested binary package into the current directory.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get clean` command leaves all retrieved package files in the cache.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get autoclean` command removes only package files that can no longer be downloaded.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get check` command automatically reinstalls packages to fix dependency problems.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get --download-only` option retrieves package files without unpacking or installing them.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: With `apt-get --only-upgrade`, `install` can install a brand new package that is not already present.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The `apt-get --no-upgrade` option prevents already installed command-line packages from being upgraded.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: When used with `upgrade`, `apt-get --with-new-pkgs` allows package removals if dependency resolution needs them.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: DNF is documented as a package manager for RPM-based Linux distributions.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: DNF exit code `0` means an error occurred but DNF handled it.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: DNF uses exit code `100` for the `check-update` case.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The DNF `--allowerasing` option forbids erasing installed packages during dependency resolution.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The DNF `--assumeno` option automatically answers no to all questions.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The DNF `--best` option lets DNF silently settle for an older directly requested package when the latest version cannot be installed.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The DNF `--cacheonly` option runs from cache without updating it, even if the cache is expired.

items.reason: Question item was loaded from the structured curated profile and reviewed individually.
items.question_text: The DNF `--disablerepo` option permanently disables repositories until they are manually re-enabled later.
